title: Hydra quota-health: cost/quota snapshot + backend-fallback state
description: Commander's quota-health signal at state/quota-health.json. Two additive, independently-written concerns share this file: (1) the operator-facing cost + quota rollup written by scripts/quota-watch.sh --write — read-only telemetry / visibility, NOT a throttle or quota gate (v1 ship principle: hard cost-control + visibility only; ticket #273); (2) the codex auto-fallback router state — active_backend, fallback_reason, fallback_expires_at, fallback_on_rate_limit, manual_override (ticket #97), written by scripts/set-backend-fallback.sh as a JSON merge. Because either writer may produce the file alone, NO field is required: an absent file, an empty {}, a cost-only file, or a fallback-only file all validate, and a file with none of the backend fields routes safely to the Claude backend. additionalProperties is true so the cost writer, the fallback writer, and any future field coexist without breaking validation. Read by Commander preflight (CLAUDE.md step 2), the quota_health block of hydra-status-output.schema.json, and scripts/select-worker-backend.sh. Specs: docs/specs/2026-06-07-quota-watch-cost-visibility.md (cost #273), docs/specs/2026-06-01-codex-auto-fallback.md (fallback #97); primitive: docs/specs/2026-04-17-codex-worker-backend.md (#96).

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp this snapshot was written. Null/absent if never written.

date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the today-counters apply to, mirrored from budget-used.json. Null when not yet populated.

wall_clock_min_today.description: Total worker wall-clock minutes today (from budget-used.json).

consecutive_rate_limit_hits.description: Consecutive rate-limit hits (from autopickup.json). 2+ drives the autopickup 2-strike auto-disable — read here for visibility only; the gate itself lives on autopickup.json and is untouched.

github_rate_limit_ok.description: Convenience flag (true when rate_limit_hits_today == 0). Mirrors the quota_health block of hydra-status-output.schema.json.

linear_rate_limit_ok.description: Convenience flag (true when rate_limit_hits_today == 0). Mirrors the quota_health block of hydra-status-output.schema.json.

total_cost_usd.description: Total cost over merged-PR tickets (from cost-per-pr.sh). Null when the cost lookup was unavailable (e.g. offline gh).

cost_per_merged_pr.description: Cost per merged PR (from cost-per-pr.sh). Null when no PRs merged or cost unavailable.

tickets_counted.description: Number of per-ticket log files cost-per-pr.sh read.

est_cost_per_ticket.description: Derived total_cost_usd / tickets_completed_today. Null when 0 tickets completed or cost unavailable.

operator_line.description: One-line operator health string consumed by the session greeting / autopickup tick.

last_rate_limit_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent rate-limit signal. Used by preflight's 'recent rate-limit' check and by `quota` reporting.

rate_limit_class.description: Classification of the last rate-limit. Only these classes may trip the codex auto-fallback; a schema/lock/network error is NOT a rate-limit and must not appear here.

active_backend.description: The backend the router currently serves. Set to 'codex' by scripts/set-backend-fallback.sh --trigger when a rate-limit fires; reverts (read-side, at TTL expiry) to the repo's preferred backend. Absent → claude.

fallback_reason.description: Why the active fallback to codex was triggered (audit / operator visibility). Mirrors rate_limit_class at trigger time.

fallback_expires_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC TTL for the active codex fallback (default now+1h). The router auto-recovers (drops back to the repo's preferred backend) once now >= this value. Read-time decision; no quota-burning probe.

fallback_on_rate_limit.description: Operator on/off switch for codex auto-fallback. Default true when absent. When false, a rate-limit records last_rate_limit_at/rate_limit_class but does NOT switch the backend (legacy 1-hr freeze behavior is retained by Commander).

manual_override.description: Operator's `backend <x>` command. 'claude'/'codex' pin the backend (beating any active auto-fallback) until 'backend auto'. 'auto' (or absent) re-enables auto-fallback. Set by scripts/set-backend-fallback.sh --set.
